channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The AOL experience is still kicking in 2025
item.description: AOL is alive and well. Decades after its heyday, AOL offers a subscription-based browser called AOL Desktop Gold, priced at $6.99 per month. While no longer tied to dial-up internet, it retains much of the charm of its late-’90s and early-2000s predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: California pulls back EPA requests for approval to phase out diesel-powered trains and big rigs
item.description: California has withdrawn requests for the EPA to give the state authority to enforce rules limiting emissions from diesel-powered trains and big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk announces a third patient has received a Neuralink brain implant
item.description: Elon Musk said a third person has received an implant from his brain-computer interface company Neuralink, one of many groups working to connect the nervous system to machines. “We’ve got . . .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arm may be planning to hike prices, has considered developing its own chips
item.description: Arm’s future company strategy revealed in still-sealed testimony and documents from December 2024's royalty spat with Qualcomm.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: DJI Mavic 4 Pro leaks show it could improve my favorite camera drone with a redesigned triple camera module
item.description: Leaked images seemingly show the rumored DJI Mavic 4 Pro's redesigned bulbous camera module which could mean improved image quality and range of motion.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: A panel of federal appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, some U.S. TikTok users are flocking to Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S. The self-described “TikTok refugees” landed on Xiaohongshu as an alternative to TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Messages finally reverses an annoying change, making it easier to organize your contacts
item.description: Google Messages users are regaining the ability to add contact names and photos for their contracts locally, following the controversial decision to implement individually-set social media-style profiles in 2024.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Opinion: The case for killing the tax credit for electric vehicles
item.description: In today's mature EV market, such credits provide tax cuts for the rich and discourage other green technologies.
item.dc:creator: Veronique de Rugy, The Mercury News

item.title: Adobe's latest Firefly AI tool will let you edit thousands of images in one go
item.description: Firefly Bulk Create is Adobe’s answer to letting you resize or change the backgrounds of thousands of images.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden to sign executive order boosting construction of AI infrastructure
item.description: President Biden will sign an executive order Tuesday aimed at reducing barriers to construct new data centers and the accompanying energy infrastructure needed to power the development of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: UK Government launches ransomware protection proposals
item.description: Hospitals, railways and other services are at risk of ransomware attacks, but new proposals look to protect them.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 10 AI strategy questions every CIO must answer
item.description: The world plunged headfirst into the AI revolution. Now many are admitting they weren’t quite ready. The 2024 Board of Directors Survey from Gartner, for example, found that 80% of non-executive directors believe their current board practices and structures are inadequate to effectively oversee AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The journey towards a knowledge graph for generative AI
item.description: How does the journey to a knowledge graph start with unstructured data—such as text, images, and other media? The evolution of web search engines offers an instructive example, showing how knowledge can be extracted from unstructured sources and refined over time into a structured, interconnected gr
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How U.S. AI chip-export curbs threaten Nvidia’s revenue
item.description: Nvidia faces a significant revenue threat due to the latest U.S. export restrictions on artificial intelligence chips, designed to limit the global distribution of these coveted processors, analysts and investors said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Joe Biden’s new chip export rules strangle competition to ensure U.S. AI supremacy
item.description: America, a country built on the principle of free markets, has decided to shut off around 120 countries from accessing the most valuable element that is powering the free market at present.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok Ban: Supreme Court Considers Law's Fate
item.description: The Supreme Court weighs the constitutionality of a law banning TikTok from operating in the US unless its owner, ByteDance, sells it by Jan 19.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times News Service Syndicate, The Mercury News

item.title: US Cybersecurity Agency Publishes Software Development Goals
item.description: The US Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has published IT sector-specific goals to protect against cyber threats, including software development process and product design goals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft plans to force-install its new Outlook app on Windows 10 PCs
item.description: Microsoft plans to force-install its new Outlook client on Windows 10 computers in conjunction with a new security update being released Feb. 11, according to Bleeping Computer. The change affects Microsoft 365 users and the new Outlook client will run alongside the classic Outlook app.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Unauthorized drone flights double; 3 arrested during LA fires
item.description: The FAA instituted temporary flight restrictions in the area. Aircraft are used to drop water on the blazes.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4
